lens.included_activities: U.S. lower-middle-market fixed-site professional car-wash operators providing conveyorized express-exterior or staffed exterior-and-interior cleaning, washing, waxing, vacuuming, and membership services to retail and fleet customers.
lens.excluded_activities: Unattended coin-operated self-service bay businesses, purely mobile hand-detailing routes, captive washes at gasoline stations, dealerships, fleets, or parking operations, equipment and chemical suppliers, household do-it-yourself washing, shells, non-control vehicles, and owner-dependent microbusinesses.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Retail and corporate customers buy single washes, prepaid packages, or recurring monthly memberships. Fixed sites combine automated payment and conveyor equipment with traffic management, customer assistance, chemistry control, equipment maintenance, site safety, and, for full-service formats, manual vacuuming and interior cleaning.
lens.deviation_from_default: Narrowed for coherence to professionally operated fixed-site conveyor formats. Unattended self-service bays and purely mobile hand-detailing routes are excluded because their capital, labor, throughput, and automation economics are opposites; the narrowing is based on model coherence rather than attractiveness.

a.bridge: BLS broad NAICS 811190 reports 67.71% of employment in transportation and material-moving occupations, including 11.40% automotive and watercraft service attendants, but the aggregation and its unusually large residual mix make it a weak exact-format proxy. Mister Car Wash shows that express customers already pay at kiosks, remain in the vehicle through an automated tunnel, and self-vacuum, while interior locations retain manual cleaning. I therefore count only not-yet-automated customer assistance, membership sales and retention, staffing, marketing, complaint handling, chemical and maintenance diagnostics, and routine administration as AI-exposed, leaving tunnel operation already automated and physical prep, interior cleaning, equipment maintenance, and site safety unexposed. The large-chain source may understate labor at smaller full-service sites and overstate existing automation, so the upper range allows a more manual mix.
a.rationale: The core exterior wash cycle and payment are already substantially automated, leaving a smaller incremental AI task pool than the industry's high labor ratio might suggest.
a.change_evidence: Exact-format task-hour studies for express exterior and interior-service locations, explicitly separating already automated tunnel work from remaining human tasks, would materially change exposure.
caveats: The occupational proxy covers oil change and other repair activities as well as car washes.
Full-service interior sites are much more labor-intensive than express-exterior sites.

rho.rationale: Fixed-site car washes already have point-of-sale, license-plate or membership records, kiosks, tunnel controls, and high transaction volumes, enabling AI in retention, promotion, staffing, chemical usage, anomaly detection, preventive maintenance, and customer support. Fragmented ownership, mixed equipment vintages, integration cost, site-level data quality, safety review, and limited independent technical staff prevent universal five-year implementation.
rho.change_evidence: Independent and chain-site cohorts showing active AI use, maintenance prediction accuracy, membership retention lift, staffing changes, and integration cost would materially revise implementation.
caveats: Existing tunnel and kiosk automation is not counted as new AI implementation.
Large chains have more data and integration capacity than typical lower-middle-market operators.

e.rationale: The exact code includes express, full-service, self-service, mobile, and detailing activities, so only part of the population fits one coherent fixed-site conveyor model. Eligibility is further reduced for captive gasoline-station or dealer washes, micro mobile operators, unattended bay assets without an operating-service organization, and firms outside the earnings band. The supplied count is establishment- and margin-bridged rather than an observed number of qualifying firms.
e.change_evidence: Firm-level format, ownership, store count, revenue mix, EBITDA, membership share, captive status, and enterprise-to-establishment links would materially change eligibility.
caveats: Formats differ materially in labor, capital, throughput, and customer proposition.
A portfolio of sites may be one firm, while one site can be held in multiple legal entities.

s5.bridge: Gallup reports that 22% of U.S. employer-business owners intended to sell, transfer, or go public within five years. I haircut the rate for noncompletion, family or employee transfers, site or real-estate sales that do not convey qualifying operating control, and formats outside the narrowed lens. The survey can overstate closed external transfers, while active greenfield development and acquisition by chains can make car-wash control transactions more frequent than the broad haircut implies.
s5.rationale: Industry consolidation supports a material transfer pool, but qualifying operating-company control sales remain a minority of eligible firms over five years.
s5.change_evidence: Closed car-wash platform and add-on transactions, owner age, broker listings, site-only versus company sales, and internal-transfer shares would materially change the estimate.
caveats: The Gallup survey is cross-industry and intention-based.
Real-estate ownership and operating control can transfer separately in this industry.

q.rationale: Operators can retain benefit through membership acquisition and churn reduction, targeted promotions, labor scheduling, chemical optimization, uptime, and faster problem resolution. Local price competition, membership discount economics, consumer cancellation, software and sensor costs, equipment maintenance, wage reassignment, and landlord or financing costs absorb a large share of the gain.
q.change_evidence: Matched-site evidence on member churn, retail conversion, washes per labor hour, chemical use, downtime, realized price, maintenance cost, and technology spending would materially change retention.
caveats: Membership growth can shift timing and frequency without increasing constant-quality value per wash.
Maintenance optimization benefits may accrue partly to equipment vendors or software providers.

d5.bridge: FHWA projects light-duty VMT growth of 0.6% annually, providing a modest five-year vehicle-use tailwind. Mister Car Wash reported 2.9% comparable-store sales growth in 2025 and net revenue rising 6% to $1.052 billion, driven by membership growth, package mix, price, and new locations; I strip out price and new-site effects and treat the comparable-store figure only as a directional proxy for wash demand. The chain's express-heavy mix and membership expansion can overstate the narrowed industry's underlying quantity, while weather, drought, household budgets, and home washing can make results weaker; subscription adoption and convenience can support stronger frequency.
d5.rationale: Vehicle use and subscription convenience support moderate volume growth, but weather, water restrictions, discretionary spending, competitive site additions, and home washing widen the range.
d5.change_evidence: Exact-industry wash counts, same-site visits, member utilization, retail tickets, deflators, weather normalization, and format-specific churn would materially change the five-year ratio.
caveats: One public chain is not representative of all regions and formats.
Revenue growth combines quantity, mix, price, memberships, and new-site effects.

o.rationale: Almost all year-five fixed-site quantity still needs an accountable operator to own or control the site and equipment, maintain the tunnel and vacuums, manage chemicals and water, ensure vehicle loading and safety, handle incidents, and provide any interior service. Unattended operation, home washing, mobile detailers, dealer or fuel-station washes, and improved vehicle coatings can displace some narrowed-lens quantity, but even highly automated tunnels require accountable maintenance and site operation.
o.change_evidence: Evidence on fully unattended conveyor sites, remote operations, robotic interior cleaning, home-wash substitution, vehicle coatings, and captive-site capture would materially change operator need.
caveats: Accountable operator need is distinct from the number of employees physically present during each wash.
Interior cleaning retains more direct operator labor than express exterior service.

narrative.executive_view: Fixed-site conveyor car washes are already heavily automated in payment and exterior cleaning, so incremental AI value lies in membership, staffing, chemistry, maintenance, and customer operations rather than replacing the wash tunnel. The lens excludes business models with opposite labor and capital structures so the primitives remain coherent.
narrative.ai_impact: AI can improve member acquisition and retention, promotion targeting, customer support, staffing, chemical usage, equipment monitoring, preventive maintenance, incident review, and manager reporting. Existing kiosks and tunnels are the automation baseline; humans remain important for vehicle loading, prep, interior cleaning, equipment repair, chemistry, site safety, and exceptions.
narrative.value_capture: Recurring memberships and local pricing allow some benefit to remain with the operator through retention, uptime, and lower variable use. Competitive site growth, discounting, cancellation, technology cost, maintenance, labor reassignment, and property economics limit retention.
narrative.firm_availability: The official category is broad, while the fixed-site conveyor lens is narrower. Establishment counts overstate firms because many operators own multiple sites, and captive, mobile, unattended, micro, or out-of-band businesses further reduce the eligible control population.
narrative.demand_durability: Light-duty travel offers a modest tailwind, and memberships can increase wash frequency. Weather, drought restrictions, household budgets, home washing, coatings, and new-site competition produce meaningful regional and cyclical risk.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The main uncertainties are format mix, already-automated versus newly AI-exposed work, one-chain demand evidence, membership frequency economics, weather normalization, site-versus-company transaction structure, and conversion of owner intentions into external control sales.
narrative.principal_driver: High-frequency transaction and equipment data can improve membership retention, labor deployment, chemical use, and tunnel uptime.
narrative.principal_weakness: The core exterior process is already automated, leaving a relatively small incremental AI task pool and a highly format-dependent firm population.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: 811192 Car Washes
sources.supports: Official definition spanning fixed-site, self-service, mobile, and detailing activities, used to define and disclose the coherence narrowing.

sources.title: May 2023 National Industry-Specific Occupational Employment and Wage Estimates: NAICS 811190
sources.supports: Broad other-automotive occupational mix used cautiously as the task and attendant-labor proxy.

sources.title: Mister Car Wash, Inc. 2025 Annual Report on Form 10-K
sources.supports: Primary-company evidence on express and interior formats, kiosks, tunnels, self-vacuuming, memberships, labor and chemicals, comparable-store sales, and revenue drivers.

sources.title: 2025 FHWA Forecasts of Vehicle Miles Traveled
sources.supports: Light-duty vehicle-use growth used as a broad demand exposure proxy.

sources.title: 2022 Economic Census: Other Services Basic Statistics
sources.supports: Exact-industry establishment and revenue context used to qualify the firm population.

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.supports: Employer-owner five-year sale and transfer intentions used as the broad starting point for the qualifying-control-transfer proxy.
